<!-- Generated by AI on 05/04/2026 -->

# Technical Specification

This is the technical specification for the spec detailed in .agent-os/specs/2026-05-04-phase-4-weekly-dashboard/spec.md

## Technical Requirements

### Weekly Reflection Domain Model

```
WeeklyReflection:
  DateTimeOffset WeekStart (Monday 00:00 UTC)
  DateTimeOffset WeekEnd (Sunday 23:59 UTC)
  int TotalThoughts
  IReadOnlyList<CategoryCount> CategoryDistribution
  IReadOnlyList<DailySentiment> DailySentimentTrend (7 items, one per day)
  IReadOnlyList<HourlyActivity> PeakActivityByHour (24 items)
  IReadOnlyList<InsightMessage> TopInsights (top 5 from InsightService)
  string? AiSummary (null if AI summary is disabled or unavailable)

CategoryCount: string Category, int Count, double Percentage
DailySentiment: DayOfWeek Day, int Positive, int Negative, int Neutral
HourlyActivity: int Hour, int ThoughtCount
```

### Weekly Reflection Aggregation Service

- Interface: `IWeeklyReflectionService` with method `Task<WeeklyReflection> GetWeeklyReflectionAsync(string userId, DateTimeOffset? weekDate = null)`
- When `weekDate` is null, compute the week containing `DateTimeOffset.UtcNow`; otherwise compute the week containing the provided date
- Week boundaries: Monday 00:00:00 UTC to Sunday 23:59:59 UTC
- Fetch all thoughts via `IThoughtRepository.GetByUserAsync`, filter to the target week, compute all aggregations in memory
- Delegates to `IInsightService` for top insights
- Registered as scoped in DI

### Optional AI Summary

- Controlled by `appsettings.json`: `AI:WeeklySummaryEnabled` (bool, default: false) and `AI:ApiKey` (string)
- Interface: `IAiSummaryService` with method `Task<string?> GenerateWeeklySummaryAsync(WeeklyReflection reflection)`
- Implementation: `OpenAiSummaryService` — constructs a structured prompt from the reflection data and calls the OpenAI Chat Completions API (or Azure OpenAI equivalent)
- If `AI:WeeklySummaryEnabled` is false or `AI:ApiKey` is empty: `NullAiSummaryService` (always returns `null`) — registered instead
- Never throws; catch all exceptions and return `null` so the rest of the reflection is unaffected
- Prompt uses only aggregated statistics — no raw thought text is sent to the external API

### API

- New controller: `ReflectionsController` in `Controllers/Reflections/ReflectionsController.cs`
- `[ApiController]`, `[Authorize]`, `[Route("api/reflections")]`
- Action: `GetWeeklyReflectionAsync` — accepts optional `?weekDate=2026-05-04` query parameter

### Frontend Charts

- Chart library: `@mui/x-charts` (part of MUI ecosystem, consistent with Paycor DS)
- **Category Distribution:** `PieChart` component — one slice per category, show count and percentage in tooltip
- **Sentiment Trend:** `LineChart` component — 3 series (Positive, Negative, Neutral), x-axis = day of week, y-axis = thought count
- **Peak Activity Heatmap:** `BarChart` component — x-axis = hour (0–23), y-axis = thought count; bars colored by density
- **Pattern Highlights:** MUI `Card` list showing top 3 insights from the weekly reflection

### Frontend Page

- New `DashboardPage` in `frontend/src/pages/DashboardPage.tsx`
- New `frontend/src/api/reflections.ts` API client with `getWeeklyReflection(weekDate?: string)` function
- Route: `/dashboard` in `App.tsx`
- `AppHeader` updated with Dashboard navigation link
- AI summary section: MUI `Typography` with summary text, or a `Chip` with label "AI Summary Not Configured" when `aiSummary` is null

## External Dependencies

- **@mui/x-charts** — MUI X Charts for React. Required for all dashboard visualizations. Install in `frontend/`.
  - **Justification:** Consistent with existing MUI/Paycor DS theme; no additional design system conflicts; actively maintained by MUI team. Using this instead of Recharts avoids a second chart library dependency.
